home.title: Motion Physics System (m/s)
home.subtitle: High-precision Rust physics engine built on Rapier3D-f64. Exposes complete API via C FFI (~480 functions) and Java JNI (~280 methods). Supports 332 tests, 5 gravity models, 3 symplectic integrators, shared-memory zero-copy Arena, and 10 solar system bodies.

gravity.title: High-Precision Gravity
gravity.desc: Spherical harmonics (EGM2008 8×8), ellipsoidal gravity, J2-J6 zonal, quadrupole tensor. Auto-selects optimal model by orbital altitude.

integrators.title: Symplectic Integrators
integrators.desc: Leapfrog, Yoshida 4th, Forest-Ruth 8th. Kahan compensated summation: 15→30 significant digits. Post-Newtonian 1PN+2PN corrections.

celestial.title: Built-in Celestial Bodies
celestial.desc: 10 solar system bodies (JPL DE441). Earth EGM2008, Moon LP165 + 12 Mascon (GRAIL), Mars Mars50c.

terrain.title: Terrain Gravity
terrain.desc: Polyhedron gravity (Werner-Scheeres), DEM mass distribution, FFT acceleration. Lunar Mascon model for low-orbit safety.

forces.title: ForceRegistry
forces.desc: Typed force registry. Any force implementing ForceLaw trait auto-dispatches during world step with aggregated reporting.

jni.title: JNI + Shared Memory
jni.desc: Java 21 JNI bindings (~280 methods). Shared memory Arena (DirectByteBuffer) with zero-JNI read/write. Lock-free event system.

cases.heading: Use Cases
cases.orbit: Case 3: Long-term Orbit with Symplectic Integrator

world._intro: PhysicsWorld is the central simulation container owning all bodies, colliders, joints, force registry, event system, and shared memory arena.

rows: Create physics world with gravity vector
Set dt, solver iterations, CCD substeps

rigid_body._intro: Rigid bodies are the fundamental dynamic objects. Each has mass, pose, velocity, and can receive forces/impulses.

collider._intro: Colliders define physics shapes. Supports standard geometry, extended bounds, and voxel systems.

rows: Merge adjacent voxels into larger boxes

query._intro: Ray casting, shape casting, point projection, and intersection tests against the physics world.

rows: Project point onto closest collider surface
Sweep a shape through the world

query.filter_code:
pub struct QueryFilterDesc {
    pub flags: u32,
    pub groups: InteractionGroupsDesc,
    pub use_groups: Bool,
    pub exclude_collider: ColliderHandleRaw,
    pub use_exclude_collider: Bool,
    pub exclude_rigid_body: RigidBodyHandleRaw,
    pub use_exclude_rigid_body: Bool,
}

gravity.mascon_text: Moon includes 12 GRAIL Mascons: Imbrium, Serenitatis, Crisium, Nectaris, Humorum, Orientale, SPA, Smythii, Fecunditatis, Tranquillitatis, Procellarum, Frigoris.
gravity.pn_text: Post-Newtonian corrections: 1PN (Mercury perihelion precession ~43"/century), 2PN (gravitational wave damping), full post_newtonian_full.

terrain.poly_text: Werner & Scheeres (1997) algorithm — exact gravity solution for arbitrary polyhedra. Ideal for irregular asteroids and comets.
terrain.dem_text: Converts Digital Elevation Models into surface mass distributions. Direct summation or FFT-accelerated. Supports arbitrary resolution.
terrain.mascon_text: 12 GRAIL mass concentrations. At 50 km lunar altitude, produces ~10⁻³ to 10⁻⁵ m/s² additional gravity — enough to crash unmodeled orbits.

forces._intro: ForceRegistry is a typed force dispatch system. Any force implementing ForceLaw trait is auto-scheduled during world_step with aggregated reporting via ForceFacade.

rows: Static + dynamic friction with velocity threshold

forces.facade_code:
pub struct ForceFacade<'a> {
    pub bodies: &'a RigidBodySet,
    pub colliders: &'a ColliderSet,
    pub gravity: &'a Vector,
    pub frame_log: &'a mut HashMap<RigidBodyHandle, BodyForceLog>,
    pub pending_forces: &'a mut SmallVec<[PendingForce; 128]>,
    pub friction_work: &'a mut Vec<(Handle, Handle, Vector)>,
    pub max_reynolds: f64,
}

rows: Start/stop of contact between two colliders
Drain all pending contact force events
Set dispatch mode: Poll / Callback / Both

spaceflight.sf_desc: 74 functions covering orbital mechanics, attitude control, thermal management, and mission operations.

sf_cards.title: Orbital Mechanics
sf_cards.desc: Keplerian/equinoctial elements, Lambert problem, Hohmann/bi-elliptic transfers, perturbation forces (J2/SRP/drag).

sf_cards.title: Attitude Control
sf_cards.desc: CMG momentum exchange, magnetic torquer dipoles, gravity gradient torque, reaction wheel models.

sf_cards.title: Thermal Control
sf_cards.desc: Stefan-Boltzmann radiation, solar flux, MLI insulation, radiator sizing, heater power.

sf_cards.title: Mission Ops
sf_cards.desc: Docking approach, GNSS visibility, debris collision probability, power budget.

spaceflight.traj_text: 6DOF ballistic trajectory with Coriolis, centrifugal, aerodynamic drag/lift, and glide integration.
spaceflight.aero_text: Surface-based aerodynamics (airfoils), voxel-based aero (block drag/lift), force estimation.
spaceflight.fluid_text: AABB fluid drag, Bernoulli pressure, SPH, simplified Navier-Stokes stepping.
spaceflight.ct_text: PID controller, state-space system, MPC box QP solver, LQR-like stable input.

astrophysics.astro_text: N-body direct summation, Barnes-Hut O(N log N), FMM monopole, post-Newtonian orbit corrections, Roche limit, orbital resonance detection.
astrophysics.chaos_text: Lorenz attractor, double pendulum, logistic map, Lyapunov exponents, bifurcation diagram, chaos detection.
astrophysics.rel_text: Lorentz transformation, four-vectors, length contraction, time dilation, Schwarzschild metric, light deflection.
astrophysics.mol_text: Lennard-Jones potential/force, Coulomb potential/force, pairwise interactions, vacuum Coulomb constant.

rows: Vlasov 方程矩 (密度/速度/温度)

arena._intro: Zero-JNI physics data access. Rust maintains a shared memory region. Java reads/writes directly via DirectByteBuffer. Only 1 JNI call per frame, 175× acceleration.
arena.sync_text: Each BodySlot has a generation counter: Rust sets it odd before writing, even after. Java checks generation is even and unchanged before/after read.
arena.buffers_text: PhysicsWorld has built-in FrameWorkBuffers — reused per-frame, eliminating 5 common heap allocation sites.
arena.drain_text: Per-body force aggregation changed from BTreeMap (heap per body per frame) to fixed-size array [Option<KahanVec3>; 32] with O(1) lookup.
arena.arch_code:
┌──────────────── Rust (shared_arena.rs) ────────────────┐
│ SharedPhysicsArena                                       │
│   header:    magic, version, flags, counts, strides      │
│   body_slots: [BodySlot; N]          ← Rust writes      │
│   body_handle_map: [u64; N]          ← Rust writes      │
│   force_report: [ForceContribution; 32] ← Rust writes   │
│   cmd_ring:   lock-free SPSC ring    ← Java writes      │
│   event_ring: lock-free SPSC ring    ← Rust writes      │
│                                                          │
│ world_step():                                            │
│   1. drain cmd_ring → process Java commands              │
│   2. ForceRegistry → execute all force laws              │
│   3. pipeline.step() → Rapier physics step               │
│   4. flush body_slots + body_handle_map                  │
│   5. flush force_report → per-ForceLawType contributions │
│   6. flush event_ring → collision/contact events         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        ↑ DirectByteBuffer (1 JNI init, Java 1.4+ API)
        ↓
┌───────────── Java (Zero JNI read/write) ─────────────────┐
│ ByteBuffer arena = worldGetArenaDirectBuffer(world);      │
│                                                           │
│ // Read body states — zero JNI                             │
│ int bodyCount = arena.getInt(32);                        │
│ double posX = arena.getDouble(128 + i*96 + 8);           │
│                                                           │
│ // Write commands — zero JNI                               │
│ arena.putDouble(cmdOff + idx*32 + 8, forceX);            │
│                                                           │
│ // Only 1 JNI trigger per frame                            │
│ RigidBodyNative.worldStep(world, 1.0 / 60.0);             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

rows: magic, version, flags, capacities, strides, live counts, offset pointers
rigidBodyAddForce(world, handle, x, y, z, wakeUp)

ecosystem.build_code:
# Local build
cargo build --release

# Output:
# target/release/mps_rigid_body.dll   (Windows)
# target/release/libmps_rigid_body.so (Linux)
# target/release/libmps_rigid_body.dylib (macOS)

# JNI deployment: copy to java.library.path or specify -Drigidbody.native.path
ecosystem.verify_code:
cargo test -p mps-test              # 332 Rust integration tests
cargo build --release               # Release build
cargo check                          # Fast syntax/type check (~2s)
cargo check -p mps-core -p mps-jni   # Individual crate checks

api._intro: All pub extern "C" fn exports (~480 functions). ✅ = JNI wrapper available.

sections.text: Total: ~480 C FFI functions, ~280 with JNI wrapper.

precision.kahan_text: Standard x += y loses low-order bits under heavy accumulation. Kahan's algorithm tracks a compensation term, boosting precision from ~15 → ~30 significant digits.
precision.f64_text: All computations use rapier3d-f64 (f64), avoiding f32 precision loss. Force accumulation uses KahanVec3.
precision.symp_text: Semi-implicit Euler does not conserve energy for gravitational systems. Symplectic integrators preserve the shadow Hamiltonian, enabling long-term orbital stability.
precision.pn_text: Earth orbit: ~10⁻⁹ relativistic correction. Mercury: ~10⁻⁷. Using 2PN formulas achieves precision to ~10⁻¹².

rows: UnsafeCell + atomic fields, no more RwLock
Merges find→Vec + per-handle unregister into one traversal
Allocation-free overload: writes into caller-provided array
